data_source: NOAA/PMEL humpback whale song via Internet Archive (WhaleSong_928, Public Domain — https://archive.org/details/WhaleSong_928); Monterey Bay Aquarium Research Institute (MBARI) humpback whale recordings via Internet Archive (humpbackwhalesongs, CC0 — https://archive.org/details/humpbackwhalesongs). 4 recordings: 13min (NOAA) + 120min + 80min + 155min (MBARI) = ~368min total. Downloaded 2026-04-11.
encoding_notes: Audio resampled to 8000Hz mono. Mel spectrogram: 32 mel bands, 20ms hop (= 0.020s frame resolution), frequency range 80-3500Hz (humpback vocal range). Per-frame energy (RMS): frames below 15th percentile discarded as silence. Active frames: dominant mel bin (argmax across 32 bands) extracted. Dominant bin (0-31) linearly mapped to 20 discrete bins, each encoded as a character A-T. Each character represents ~20ms of audio (~20 pitch regions from 80Hz to 3500Hz). HIGH structure score expected: humpback songs are hierarchically organised — units -> phrases -> themes -> songs — with strong sequential repetition.

audio_files.description: NOAA humpback whale song (13min, 44.1kHz, Public Domain)

audio_processing.encoding: dominant mel bin (argmax) per active frame, linearly mapped to N_BINS
